jokes.text: Nothing, we merely exchanged familial smiles. He seemed quite taken with an older woman, so I thought best not to intrude.
jokes.setup: Alexis asks what Ted said when Moira saw him
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly formal description of Ted being with someone, calling her 'older woman'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - overly formal language about a simple situation, 'familial smiles' is wonderfully pretentious

jokes.text: They were canoodling in the banquette for all to see, so I'd be rather concerned if that were his mother, David.
jokes.setup: David suggests the older woman could be Ted's mother or close friend
jokes.punchline: Moira's dry observation about why that would be concerning given the 'canoodling'
jokes.explanation: 'Canoodling in the banquette' is peak Moira vocabulary, plus the deadpan logic is perfect

jokes.text: No, no braid. Uh, looked more like thick, healthy hair woven together loosely.
jokes.setup: Alexis asks if the woman had a braid
jokes.punchline: Moira describes a braid without calling it a braid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira's pretentious vocabulary prevents her from using simple words even when they're more accurate

jokes.text: Ok, um, but you're in like a day dress. Mhmm, I am.
jokes.setup: Alexis announces she's going for a jog
jokes.punchline: David points out she's inappropriately dressed, Alexis doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag enhanced by Alexis's nonchalant response showing her obliviousness

jokes.text: Okay, I have never heard someone say so many wrong things, one after the other, consecutively, in a row.
jokes.setup: Moira has been saying insensitive things about Ted and Heather
jokes.punchline: David's frustrated enumeration of how wrong Moira was, with redundant phrasing
jokes.explanation: Great David line - the redundant phrasing 'one after the other, consecutively, in a row' shows his exasperation building

jokes.text: Striking, I believe was the word you used to describe Ted's new girlfriend, who isn't Alexis.
jokes.setup: Moira says she called the other woman charming
jokes.punchline: David's dry correction emphasizing the hurtfulness with 'who isn't Alexis'
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - protective of Alexis but delivered with his signature dry precision

jokes.text: Well to my credit, she was.
jokes.setup: David corrects Moira about calling Ted's girlfriend striking
jokes.punchline: Moira doubles down on the insensitive comment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly clueless Moira - completely missing the point and making it worse with 'to my credit'

jokes.text: Beer fridge reveal and Roland's casual setup
jokes.setup: Roland is talking about wasted space in the office
jokes.punchline: He reveals he's already installed a beer fridge without permission
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing Roland's presumption and boundary violations

jokes.text: Joce wanted a little extra space for the new baby, and I thought why not help out a buddy in the process?
jokes.setup: Johnny asks how bringing the fridge helps him
jokes.punchline: Roland frames his selfish act as helping both his wife and Johnny
jokes.explanation: Roland's twisted logic - making his selfish move sound altruistic

jokes.text: Would that staff member be you? Yes, it would. I'm gonna have 'em after lunch.
jokes.setup: Roland brags about selling beers to a staff member
jokes.punchline: Reveals he bought them himself and hasn't even drunk them yet
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Roland's 'business success' gets progressively more pathetic

jokes.text: Oh God... you knock me out, kid.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks where the TV would go
jokes.punchline: Roland treats Johnny's obvious frustration as endearing humor
jokes.explanation: Roland completely misreading the room - thinks Johnny's exasperation is wit

jokes.text: I cursed those bears with the little strength that I could muster up.
jokes.setup: Patrick enters mid-conversation
jokes.punchline: Random comment about cursing bears reveals Patrick's odd small talk
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Patrick moment - the specificity of 'little strength that I could muster' is perfect

jokes.text: Well, it's not exactly before work the store opened about 25 minutes ago, so. But yes, there is a chain, and no, you're not on it.
jokes.setup: David asks if there was a text chain he wasn't on
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutal honesty about excluding David
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - brutally honest delivery with perfect timing, enhanced by Patrick's matter-of-fact addition

jokes.text: Well, it's not exactly before work the store opened about 25 minutes ago, so.
jokes.setup: David asks if they're hanging out before work
jokes.punchline: Patrick's matter-of-fact revelation that they're quite late
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of David's lateness delivered in Patrick's characteristic understated style.

jokes.text: No, they're just new mints that haven't been sampled yet, so for all we know, they could be poison
jokes.setup: Patrick asks what's wrong with moving the lip balms for new mints
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic escalation to poison
jokes.explanation: Classic David catastrophizing - jumping from untested mints to poison shows his dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: I just wish they had been consulted before they were moved.
jokes.setup: David explaining his issue with moving the lip balms
jokes.punchline: Talking about lip balms as if they're sentient beings needing consultation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David moment - anthropomorphizing products while avoiding admitting he just doesn't like change

jokes.text: Okay, well they were fugly brooms with big red handles. They didn't match our sand and stone colour palette.
jokes.setup: Patrick points out David moves things without consulting him
jokes.punchline: David's passionate defense using 'fugly' and specific design language
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - 'fugly' mixed with pretentious design terminology, plus the passion for aesthetics

jokes.text: What? Nothing, I just um, just remembering all those times that you compromised. I was just thinking about the same things, because there, there's so many to flip through.
jokes.setup: David claims he compromises all the time
jokes.punchline: Stevie and Patrick's sarcastic 'remembering' of non-existent compromises
jokes.explanation: Great team-up moment with layered sarcasm - 'so many to flip through' is perfectly cutting

jokes.text: You made me pick between two Sandra Bullock vehicles.
jokes.setup: David claims letting Patrick pick the movie was a compromise
jokes.punchline: Patrick reveals the 'choice' was between two movies David wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of false choice - 'vehicles' is a funny formal way to say movies

jokes.text: Just so that you know, making someone choose between two things that you like, is not exactly a compromise.
jokes.setup: David defends his movie choice method
jokes.punchline: Patrick's patient explanation of what compromise actually means
jokes.explanation: Patrick's patient, logical takedown of David's flawed reasoning

jokes.text: So you guys are just gonna stay behind and talk about me after I've left. You know, swap stories about how I don't compromise.
jokes.setup: David is leaving to get tote bags
jokes.punchline: David's paranoid assumption about what they'll discuss
jokes.explanation: David's insecurity showing through paranoia - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Yeah, pretty much.
jokes.setup: David asks if they'll talk about him
jokes.punchline: Their immediate, honest confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and honesty - the immediate confirmation is brutal and funny

jokes.text: Always the little philanthrope. Though it's possible this outing might serve you both well.
jokes.setup: Twyla explains Alexis insisted they go out
jokes.punchline: Moira's mispronunciation of 'philanthropist' and subtle dig
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism with a hint of condescension - 'philanthrope' is wonderfully wrong

jokes.text: A closed book that falls open the second you take it off the shelf.
jokes.setup: Moira calls Alexis a closed book, Twyla says she's open
jokes.punchline: Moira's elaborate metaphor for Alexis being an over-sharer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira metaphor - captures Alexis perfectly while being hilariously overwrought

jokes.text: Gossip is the devil's telephone. Best to just hang up.
jokes.setup: Twyla tries to clarify she wasn't gossiping
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic pronouncement about gossip
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira wisdom - profound-sounding nonsense delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: Not the movie! This!
jokes.setup: Roland explains he's showing guests Erin Brockovich setup
jokes.punchline: Johnny's frustrated clarification about the real situation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's exasperation at Roland's obliviousness about what the actual problem is

jokes.text: I dunno, maybe it's your dry wit, you know, that never quite hits the target.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks why Roland thought he was joking about no TV
jokes.punchline: Roland's casual insult about Johnny's humor
jokes.explanation: Roland being obliviously insulting while missing the point entirely

jokes.text: Although they could be friends too, I don't know, they could be brothers and friends. Guys, how would you describe your relationship?
jokes.setup: Roland mentions the guests are brothers
jokes.punchline: Roland's overthinking of their relationship and asking them directly
jokes.explanation: Roland's tendency to overthink simple things and involve strangers inappropriately

jokes.text: you insist on keeping them hidden away in the back room because you find them offensive.
jokes.setup: Patrick explains why he moved the plungers
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of David's ridiculous attitude toward plungers
jokes.explanation: Patrick's deadpan acknowledgment of David's absurd priorities - finding plungers 'offensive'

jokes.text: They don't allow you to drink and drive anymore.
jokes.setup: Discussing how they'll get to the bar
jokes.punchline: Moira's casual reference to drunk driving like it's a recent policy change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - out of touch with reality, 'anymore' suggests she's bitter about this 'new' rule

jokes.text: If, and when, you meet someone who catches your eye, hold his gaze. Then walk up behind him, trace a single finger down his back.
jokes.setup: Moira offering dating advice
jokes.punchline: Her specific and inappropriate seduction technique
jokes.explanation: Moira's dating advice is creepy and specific - the finger tracing is particularly uncomfortable

jokes.text: Hm, that sounds super embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Moira's elaborate seduction advice
jokes.punchline: Alexis's understated rejection of the creepy advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated response to Moira's over-the-top advice

jokes.text: John Cougar... No. Mellencamp. But guess who drove me home that night? Mr. Rose. Yes, you're right.
jokes.setup: Moira builds up story about targeting John Cougar Mellencamp
jokes.punchline: The anticlimax that she went home with Johnny instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - builds expectation of wild celebrity story then deflates to sweet domestic reality

jokes.text: John Cougar... No. Mellencamp.
jokes.setup: Moira teases about her last target for the finger technique
jokes.punchline: The buildup and revelation of John Cougar Mellencamp
jokes.explanation: Good buildup with Alexis's interruption making the reveal funnier

jokes.text: Alexis! You're wrinkled in the back.
jokes.setup: The girls are leaving the car
jokes.punchline: Moira's last-minute appearance concern
jokes.explanation: Typical mother moment but enhanced by Moira's particular phrasing

jokes.text: I have just remembered how attentive Roland gets when I am pregnant. He is very hands on, very concerned, and he is just always... there.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining why Roland should stay at work
jokes.punchline: Her description of Roland's overwhelming pregnancy attention
jokes.explanation: The pause before 'there' and Jocelyn's delivery makes Roland's clinginess uncomfortable

jokes.text: Wow! I just upgraded my wand! Two more levels and I'll have enough opals to buy the harp.
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about Roland avoiding his pregnant wife
jokes.punchline: Roland's enthusiastic gaming commentary interrupting the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Roland's oblivious gaming excitement during serious adult conversation

jokes.text: Alone time, Johnny. I just need some alone time.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asking Johnny to keep Roland at work longer
jokes.punchline: Her desperate repetition emphasizing her need to escape Roland
jokes.explanation: The repetition and desperation in Jocelyn's voice makes this both funny and sad

jokes.text: Your mom's trick totally worked. I ran my finger down the backs of three different guys, and one of them turned around.
jokes.setup: Twyla reporting success with Moira's dating advice
jokes.punchline: Revelation that she had to try it three times before getting one response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that makes Moira's advice even funnier - 'one of them turned around' is perfectly deadpan

jokes.text: Just joking, you've got Ted's voicemail. Don't you hate it when people do that? Me too, and yet I just did it.
jokes.setup: Alexis calls Ted and gets his voicemail
jokes.punchline: Ted's self-aware voicemail message about fake-out greetings
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that's perfectly in character for Ted's awkward charm

jokes.text: Like Beyonce, I excel as a solo artist, and I was also dressed by my mother well into my teens, okay?
jokes.setup: David finally admitting he's bad at compromise
jokes.punchline: His bizarre comparison to Beyonce plus the personal overshare
jokes.explanation: Peak David moment - the Beyonce comparison is absurd, plus the vulnerable overshare makes it perfect

jokes.text: These mountaineering shoes that my boyfriend is wearing, looking like Oprah on a Thanksgiving Day hike, incorrect.
jokes.setup: David listing things that are incorrect
jokes.punchline: His specific and brutal description of Patrick's shoes
jokes.explanation: 'Oprah on a Thanksgiving Day hike' is brilliantly specific and vivid - classic David imagery

jokes.text: I think it was something about your boyfriend's shoes? Um... I don't remember saying that.
jokes.setup: Patrick catches David calling him his boyfriend
jokes.punchline: David's immediate denial despite being caught red-handed
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship moment disguised as comedy - David's panic at the accidental admission

jokes.text: My boyfriend doesn't like the shoes, so I'm gonna take the shoes off.
jokes.setup: David denying he said anything about boyfriends
jokes.punchline: Patrick embracing the boyfriend label and David's criticism
jokes.explanation: Sweet and funny - Patrick's gentle acceptance of both the relationship and David's opinions

jokes.text: sock feet in a public place is also incorrect.
jokes.setup: Patrick is taking off his shoes
jokes.punchline: David's continued critique even when Patrick is trying to accommodate him
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - even when people try to please him, he finds new problems

jokes.text: Bored, so I found a lovely little speakeasy at the back of the club.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining where she was while waiting
jokes.punchline: Her pretentious description of what's probably just the back of the bar
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pretension - making a basic bar sound sophisticated and exclusive

jokes.text: there was a reason I drew Mr. Cougar Mellencamp into that dark corner of the bar instead of your father.
jokes.setup: Moira about to tell a story about Johnny
jokes.punchline: Reference back to the John Cougar Mellencamp story with new context
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds new context to the earlier JCM story

jokes.text: It took a year. A year? But if it's meant to be, they'll come around!
jokes.setup: Moira explaining how long it took with Johnny
jokes.punchline: Alexis's shock at the timeline and Moira's optimistic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good mother-daughter moment with Alexis's horror at the wait time being relatable

jokes.text: I'm driving? That's a good idea!
jokes.setup: They need to get home from the bar
jokes.punchline: Drunk Moira enthusiastically agrees that Alexis should drive
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about drunk person's impaired judgment about driving safety
